title: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple
tagline: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India, dedicated to Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Lord Vishnu.
overview.description: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India, dedicated to Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Lord Vishnu. The temple is glorified by Maharishi Valmiki and classified as one of the 108 Abhimana Kshethrams of the Vaishnavate tradition. The town of Mudikondan is located at a distance of 20 kilometres from Mayiladuthurai and 15 kilometres from Thiruvarur in the Nannilam taluk.This temple one of Pancha Rama Kshethrams. All these temples are said to be particular locations mentioned in Ramayana.

topPlaces.name: Alangudi, Nannilam
topPlaces.description: Alangudi is a village in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sannanallur
topPlaces.description: Sannanallur is a area in Nannilam Town in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu and part of Nannilam Taluk. Until the early 1990s, the village was part of Thanjavur district, prior to it being split into three new districts.

topPlaces.name: Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple (Tamil: திருக்கொண்டீஸ்வரம் பசுபதீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirukondeeswaram in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Pasupatheeswarar.

topPlaces.name: Koothanur Maha Saraswathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Koothanur Maha Saraswathi Temple is a Hindu temple located in the town of Koothanur in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is dedicated to Saraswathi, the Hindu goddess of learning.

topPlaces.name: Adhalaiyur
topPlaces.description: Adhalaiyur is a village in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu State of India.

topPlaces.name: Nannilam block
topPlaces.description: Nannilam block is a revenue block in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 48 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Nannilam taluk
topPlaces.description: Nannilam taluk is a taluk in Thiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Nannilam.

topPlaces.name: Enangudi
topPlaces.description: Enangudi is the main village which is unified with neighbouring villages like Adalaiyur, Karipakkam, Ketharimangalam, Putthagaram, Kayathoor, Karuppuoor, Thepiramangalam, Vawvaladi and Pakkam Kottor. The nearest town of significant size is Tiruvarur which is 16 km away, Mayiladuthurai is 30 km and Nagapattinam is around 28 km away.

items.note: Alangudi is a village in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district in Tamil Nadu, India.

Sannanallur is a area in Nannilam Town in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu and part of Nannilam Taluk. Until the early 1990s, the village was part of Thanjavur district, prior to it being split into three new districts.

Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple (Tamil: திருக்கொண்டீஸ்வரம் பசுபதீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirukondeeswaram in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Pasupatheeswarar.

Adhalaiyur is a village in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu State of India.
Nannilam block is a revenue block in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 48 panchayat villages.
Nannilam taluk is a taluk in Thiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Nannilam.

Enangudi is the main village which is unified with neighbouring villages like Adalaiyur, Karipakkam, Ketharimangalam, Putthagaram, Kayathoor, Karuppuoor, Thepiramangalam, Vawvaladi and Pakkam Kottor. The nearest town of significant size is Tiruvarur which is 16 km away, Mayiladuthurai is 30 km and Nagapattinam is around 28 km away.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Alangudi, Nannilam, Sannanallur, Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple, Koothanur Maha Saraswathi Temple, Adhalaiyur.
